2004-11-01.title: Both Parties Are Getting Out the U.S. Vote in Israel
2004-11-01.abstract: Estimated 100,000 potential American voters live in Israel, many of them from critical swing states like Florida, Ohio, Michigan and Pennsylvania; estimated 10,000 Palestinian-Americans living in West Bank and Gaza Strip are eligible to vote; Republicans and Democrats are running voter registration campaigns, sometimes on Internet, hoping to capitalize on surge in voter interest this year; in last several presidential elections, Jewish voters in Israel favored Democratic candidate over Republican by 80 percent to 20 percent; Pres Bush is expected to make much stronger showing because of his support for Israel (M)

2004-11-01.title: Concern Rises in Pakistan of a War Without End
2004-11-01.abstract: Pakistan's leadership faces quandary over American-led antiterror effort; while army and government are enjoying fruits of billions of dollars in American aid, they are hunted by Islamic militants themselves; Pres Pervez Musharraf has narrowly escaped two assassination attempts; no one is quite sure what policies, if any, would end support for Islamic militancy in Pakistan, and there is fear that American policies are actually fueling that militancy; stalemate has emerged, with militants unable to carry out spectacular attacks, but government unable to eradicate militants (M)

2004-11-01.title: Sweep Expected in Venezuela Vote
2004-11-01.abstract: Allies of Venezuelan Pres Hugo Chavez expect to sweep crucial posts in nationwide regional elections on Oct 31; opposition is expected to lose as many as 100 mayoral posts, including one in Caracas, and as many as five governorships; opposition has been struggling to remain relevant since losing referendum to recall Chavez in Aug (M)

2004-11-01.title: Afghan Militants Release Video of Hostages
2004-11-01.abstract: Militants holding three foreign United Nations staff members in Afghanistan release videotape showing their hostages; say three will be killed if Muslim prisoners in Afghanistan and Guantanamo Bay are not released and foreign troops withdrawn from Afghanistan by Nov 3; video, broadcast by Al Jazeera, is strongest indication that Jaish-e-Muslimeen, breakaway group of Taliban, is adopting media-oriented tactics of kidnapping groups in Iraq; photo (M)

2004-11-01.title: Arafat's Absence Raises Issue of How to Cope
2004-11-01.abstract: Israeli cabinet and Palestinian officials hold separate gatherings dealing with same question: how to respond to absence of Yasir Arafat, who remains hospitalized in France with undiagnosed illness; Prime Min Ariel Sharon says Arafat's absence will not alter plans for unilateral Israeli withdrawal of soldiers and Jewish settlers from Gaza Strip next year; Sharon, who has refused to deal with Arafat, raises possibility that new Palestinian leadership could open door to negotiations envisioned under Middle East peace plan known as road map that collapsed shortly after it was introduced last year; Palestinian officials seek to demonstrate that Palestinian institutions are still working, but they do not want to give impression they are usurping power while Arafat is away; photos (M)

2004-11-02.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Refugee Chief Says Israel Incites Distrust
2004-11-02.abstract: Peter Hansen, United Nations refugee agency for Palestinians director, says Israel is trying to discredit agency work and requests apology for misleading statements, which could place agency workers at risk of increased hostility; UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan reiterates full support of Hansen's work (S)

2004-11-02.title: Moves toward a more federal Europe may be out of step with voters' wishes : Despite EU victories, forces of disunity
World Briefing | Asia: Uzbekistan: Several Thousand Protest
2004-11-02.abstract: Thousands of Uzbekistan citizens demonstrate in protest of new import laws in Kokand; authorities report that demonstration, which is rare in authoritarian Uzbekistan, was dispersed peacefully (S)

2004-11-02.title: Haiti's Political Vacuum Stokes Flames of Gang Violence
2004-11-02.abstract: Heafily-armed Haitian gangs that had been united behind former president Jean-Bertrand Aristide have viciously turned against one another, catching tems of thousands of ordinary citizens in cross-fire; death toll in Port-au-Prince's poorest slum, Cite-Soleil, is impossible to know because police will not enter; photos; map (M)

2004-11-03.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: A Pack Rat With Plutonium
2004-11-03.abstract: Retired Soviet physicist Leonid Grigorov hands over eight small containers of plutonium-238 to Altai regional authorities after learning of reward being offered for weapons; holds he found material in laboratory scrap heap in 1997 or 1998 and has kept it in his garage; type of plutonium could be used in making dirty-bomb (S)

2004-11-03.title: Macedonian church guards its identity : Bishop's struggle reflects wider Balkan rift
Note links beheading to deaths of 84 Thais
Waiting, watching and losing sleep : On edge in Europe, too
Note calls beheading revenge for deaths of Muslim Thais

2004-11-04.title: Bush wins 2nd term by a solid margin : Kerry failed to connect on economy
Election of Karzai Is Declared Official
2004-11-04.abstract: Pres Hamid Karzai is formally declared winner of Oct 9 presidential election by Afghanistan's electoral board after international panel announces that irregularities it found in voting would not change overall result; complaints of fraud by most of Karzai's 17 opponents detract from his victory as country's first directly elected head of state; his success is also overshadowed by kidnapping of three foreign United Nations election workers in Kabul last week by Jaish-e-Muslimeen, which threatens to kill them if its demands are not met (M)

2004-11-04.title: G.I. Deserter Tells of Cold, Hungry Times in North Korea
2004-11-04.abstract: Charles Robert Jenkins, American Army sergeant who left his soldiers and walked into North Korea in 1965, receives light sentence after pleading guilty in court-martial held at Camp Zama in Japan; he is demoted to private, stripped of four decades of back pay and benefits, given dishonorable discharge and 30-day jail sentence; trial and sentencing seen relecting American political needs to mollify Japanese public opinion, which has been moved by drama of American defector and his Japanese wife, whom he met in North Korea after her abduction from Japan by North Korean agents; Jenkins apologizes to soldiers under his command, to Army and to United States; photo (M)

2004-11-04.title: Iraqi Leader Appeals to France and Germany for Aid
2004-11-04.abstract: Iraq's interim prime minster appealed to what he called the "spectators" in the war in Iraq to become more involved.

2004-11-04.title: Former separatist leader sends a letter : A plea to Spain's rebels

2004-11-05.title: Iraqi Leader Asks Help of 'Spectator' Nations
2004-11-05.abstract: Prime Min Ayad Allawi of Iraq, on trip to Europe, appeals to what he calls 'spectator' countries in war in Iraq--specifically France and Germany--to become more involved in creating peace and prosperity there; he is scheduled to meet in Brussels with French Pres Jacques Chirac and German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder in what could be early test of any changing positions in Europe after Pres Bush's re-election; Allawi contends that even countries that opposed war are encouraged to build commercial relationship with Iraq based on reciprocal interests; in Rome, Allawi meets with Italian Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi, who repeats his pledge to keep 3,000 Italian troops deployed in Iraq; Allawi also meets with Pope John Paul II, who issues statement encouraging efforts of Iraqi people to establish democratic institutions; photo (M)

2004-11-05.title: Ivory Coast Cease-Fire Ends With Airstrikes Against 2 Rebel Towns
2004-11-05.abstract: Government planes in Ivory Coast conduct bombing raids against two rebel-held towns, ending tenuous yearlong cease-fire and signaling possible resumption of civil war; United Nations Secretary General Kofi Annan calls airstrikes 'major violation' of cease-fire and warns Pres Laurent Gbagbo and rebels against further hostilities; 6,000 UN peacekeepers and 4,500 French troops are posted in Ivory Coast, charged with keeping warring parties at bay; map (M)

2004-11-05.title: World Briefing | Asia: Taiwan: Court Backs President
2004-11-05.abstract: The High Court ruled that a recount of the disputed presidential election last March had confirmed that President Chen Shui-bian won re-election. The court dismissed a Nationalist Party lawsuit contending that the election results should be overturned because of questions about a shooting incident in which the president was injured on the eve of the election -- an incident that may have elicited sympathy votes for him. Lien Chan, the defeated Nationalist candidate, vowed an appeal. Another Nationalist lawsuit, citing voting irregularities, is still pending.   Keith Bradsher (NYT)

2004-11-05.title: World Briefing | Americas: Venezuela: Chávez Allies Take 21 Of 23 States
2004-11-05.abstract: Election authorities said that the governor of Carabobo, a state that has been a bastion for anti-government forces, had lost his seat to a pro-government challenger in the regional elections on Sunday, cementing President Hugo Chávez's political dominance of the country. The victory by Luis Acosta, a former general, in the tight race against the incumbent, Henrique Salas, means that Mr. Chávez's allies swept nearly all the opposition candidates from important offices. Anti-government governors in six of eight states controlled by the opposition were voted out, leaving pro-Chávez allies in charge of 21 of Venezuela's 23 states. A pro-government mayor also took control of Caracas, which had been in opposition hands.   Juan Forero (NYT)

2004-11-05.title: British and French Leaders Differ on Impact of Bush Victory for Europe
2004-11-05.abstract: Assembled leaders appeared to back away from proposed changes to resuscitate Europe's economy.

2004-11-06.title: U.N. Official Says Iraqi Vote Is Proceeding on Schedule
2004-11-06.abstract: Carina Perelli, chief UN electoral expert, gives upbeat report on elections scheduled for Jan 2005 in Iraq; says that despite difficulties of working in such conflicted conditions, procedures are advancing on schedule (M)

2004-11-06.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Wal-Mart Opens Near Pyramids
2004-11-06.abstract: Wal-Mart discount store opens near Teotihuacan pyramids after months of protests; locals appear more interested in possible employment and better prices than preserving archaeological heritage of area around 1,800-year-old pyramids (S)

2004-11-06.title: Spanish honor author, but remain aloof : Like tilting at a windmill:Cervantes lost on Madrid
Palestinian Politicians Try to Head Off a Power Struggle
2004-11-06.abstract: Leading Palestinian politicians are moving rapidly to prevent visible vacuum of power with Yasir Arafat apparently near death; are reaching out to disaffected younger legislators, Palestinian militant organizations and Israelis; photo (M)

2004-11-06.title: Names of the Dead
2004-11-06.abstract: Defense Department confirms deaths of two marines in Iraq

2004-11-07.title: Names of the Dead
2004-11-07.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of one American soldier in Iraq, bringing total to 1,123

2004-11-07.title: Palestinian Leaders to Visit Arafat's Doctors
2004-11-07.abstract: Yasir Arafat's deputy in the Palestine Liberation Organization, Mahmoud Abbas, and the Palestinian prime minister, Ahmed Qurie, will go to France on Monday.

2004-11-07.title: Palestinian Line of Succession Answers and Raises Questions
2004-11-07.abstract: Palestinian law, in event Yasir Arafat cannot serve because of death or illness, calls for speaker of parliament to become caretaker leader of Palestinian Authority until elections, which must be held within two months; obstacles to elections may cause Palestinian Authority to ignore law and simply appoint successor, who is likely to be Mahmoud Abbas; another possibility is collective leadership, with elections postponed; municipal elections are scheduled for December (M)

2004-11-07.title: Europe, Seeking Political Unity, Stumbles Over Issue of Religion
2004-11-07.abstract: Constitution signed by European Union leaders makes no mention of Europe's Christian roots; strong secularism has dominated European political discourse for decades, but now rare debate has opened over social issues as many conservative critics charge that secularism is getting out of hand (M)

2004-11-07.title: Afghan Militants Hold Talks on Hostages, but No Deal Yet
2004-11-07.abstract: Negotiations with officials from the Afghan government and the United Nations ended without result.

2004-11-08.title: G.I.'s Open Attack to Take Falluja From Iraq Rebels
2004-11-08.abstract: Explosions and heavy gunfire thunder across Falluja as American troops seize control of two strategic bridges, hospital and other objectives in first stage of long-expected invasion of city, center of Iraqi insurgency; Prime Min Ayad Allawi proclaims state of emergency for 60 days, which gives him broad powers to impose curfews, order house-to-house searches and detain suspects; force is composed of between 10,000 and 15,000 American soldiers and marines backed by newly trained Iraqi forces; American commanders brace troops for brutal block-by-block battle to take control and capture, kill or disperse estimated 3,000 to 4,000 insurgents; most civilians in Falluja, city of about 250,000, are believed to have left; Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld and Gen Richard Myers are monitoring combat preparation and updated combat reports; photos; maps (M)

2004-11-08.title: More French Troops Are Sent to Ivory Coast as Violence Flares
2004-11-08.abstract: French Defense Min Michele Alliot-Marie announces deployment of additional 600 troops to Ivory Coast after loyalist citizens rebel in streets of Abidjan and attack French citizens and French-owned businesses; Pres Lauren Gbagbo says he ordered army offensive in north halted and urges demonstrators to stop violent protests and attacks; United Nations Security Council is meeting to discuss possible penalties against government for breaking cease-fire and allowing violence; loyalists accuse French of aiding rebel insurgency (M)

2004-11-08.title: Paris tries to rein in Ivory Coast
East Germans Ambivalent 15 Years After Fall of Berlin Wall
2004-11-08.abstract: Many young people have quit eastern Germany to escape the high unemployment, running at over 18 percent.

2004-11-08.title: Afghan Militants Conduct Talks Over 3 Hostages
2004-11-08.abstract: Jaish-e-Muslimeen, Taliban splinter group holding United Nations workers hostage in Afghanistan, send spokesman to negotiate with Afghan officials and United Nations representatives in southern Afghanistan; hand over list of 25 prisoners whom they want released; hostages, who were kidnapped on Oct 28, are British-Irish woman Annetta Flanigan, Kosovo Albanian woman Shqipe Habibi and Filipino Angelito Nayan; negotiations to secure release of captives have not been successful (S)

2004-11-09.title: 7 in Family Slain in Mexico in Mix-Up Laid to Police Renegades
2004-11-09.abstract: Seven people returning home after Nocupetaro religious festival are gunned down by assassins apparently mistaking them for someone else; three children, 5-year-old Guadalupe Hernandez and 14-year-olds Armando Hernandez and Maria Hernandez Figueroa, and four adults are killed; Michoacan State Attorney General Miguel Angel Arellano Pulido reports that two local police officers have been taken into custody for involvement in shooting (S)

2004-11-09.title: World Briefing | Americas: Nicaragua: Sandinistas' Strong Showing
2004-11-09.abstract: Left-wing Sandinista Party gains influence in country after weekend municipal elections; party now controls 15 of 17 provincial capitals, including Managua; Pres Enrique Bolanos party appears to be losing support amid allegations that leader accepted illegal campaign contributions (S)

2004-11-09.title: A membership issue:billions in purchases : Turkey's economic attraction to EU
Report Heightens Pollution Dispute at Indonesian Bay
2004-11-09.abstract: Indonesian government panel presents report on water quality in Buyat Bay, which residents and some environmental experts hold has been toxically polluted by Newmont Mining Corporation, world's biggest gold producer; report holds that although mine waste deposited is polluted with significant levels of arsenic and mercury, water quality meets Indonesian standards; villagers, who have filed $543 million lawsuit against Newmont, contend that they suffer waste related health problems as direct result of bay contamination; Dave A Baker, Newmont vice pres for environment affairs, says that mine is not causing harm to environment, but has shut down operation there; levels of contaminates debated; Indonesian journalist suggests Newmont tried to bribe reporters at news conference on recent findings after envelope of money was found in press kit; Newmont holds money was travel reimbursement; map; drawing; photo (L)

2004-11-09.title: Diana's Park Memorial Today: A Font of Faultfinding
2004-11-09.abstract: Diana, Princess of Wales Memorial Fountain, which was designed by American landscape architect Kathryn Gustafson and opened in July in Hyde Park, London, has been plagued with problems; $6.7 million Mobius strip carved of Welsh granite requires constant patrolling by brigade of guards, who are busy enforcing rules and straining leaves and sticks from water; Parks Department holds that fountain and surroundings are not ready for thousands of visitors, but many people are critical of design; fountain has been closed several times and changes continue to be made to improve function and safety; photo (M)

2004-11-10.title: Names of the Dead
2004-11-10.abstract: United States Defense Department confirms deaths of four American service members who died in Iraq (S)

2004-11-10.title: World Briefing | Asia: Nepal: Katmandu Bomb Injures 37
2004-11-10.abstract: Two separate bombs thought to be set by Maoist rebels injure 39 people in Katmandu and Bhaktapur, Nepal; security analysts hold attacks are meant to show that recent arrest of Maoist leader Comrade Prachanda by government forces has not had effect on insurgency (S)

2004-11-10.title: In Mourning Slain Filmmaker, Dutch Confront Limitations of Their Tolerance
2004-11-10.abstract: Amsterdam funeral for Dutch filmmaker Theo van Gogh, who was killed last week by Muslim extremist, polarizes national debate over conservative Islam in nation that is traditionally tolerant; public anger has risen since killing and several retaliatory actions by both Muslims and opponents to Islam have taken place, including bombing of Eindhoven Muslim elementary school, suspicious fire at Uden elementary school and attempts to burn down Protestant churches in Rotterdam, Utrecht and Amersfoort; anti-immigration movement, which began in 2001 and was spearheaded by Pim Fortuyn, who was killed by activist in 2002, has grown in force and may inhibit discussion about political change and religious freedoms; van Gogh's killing is, at least for now, roadblock to discussion on national unity; photos (L)

2004-11-10.title: Powell, in Mexico, Pledges Migrant Reform
2004-11-10.abstract: United States Sec of State Colin L Powell, speaking at news conference during visit with Mexican Foreign Minister Luis Ernesto Derbez, assures Mexican officials that Pres George W Bush is committed to pursuing temporary worker program in US; admits, however, that plan, which gives temporary resident status to some illegal aliens holding jobs in US, will need support of Congress to become law (S)

2004-11-10.title: Sunni Party Leaves Iraqi Government Over Falluja Attack
2004-11-10.abstract: Iraqi Islamic Party, Iraq's most prominent Sunni political party, withdraws from interim government after United States and Iraqi forces invade Falluja; leading Sunni clerics also call for boycott of national elections scheduled for Jan 2005; boycott of Sunnis, who make up 20 percent of population, could undermine legitimacy of election and new government; although many Sunni leaders would have preferred Falluja be secured through peaceful negotiations, prominent Sunni politician Hajim Al Hassani admits no one has come up with non-violent solution to overcome insurgency in Falluja and ensure safety of region; political fallout from attack on Falluja is uncertain as battle there continues; photo (M)

2004-11-11.title: Bosnian Serbs Apologize for Srebrenica Massacre
2004-11-11.abstract: Bosnian Serb authorities issue, for first time, official apology for 1995 Srebrenica massacre of estimated 8,000 Muslim men and boys; statement comes after intense international pressure to accept responsibility for incident and renew efforts to bring those involved to trial (S)

2004-11-11.title: Yasir Arafat, Father and Leader of Palestinian Nationalism, Dies at 75
2004-11-11.abstract: Palestinian nationalist, guerrilla leader and Nobel Peace Prize winner Yasir Arafat dies at age 75; was divisive figure central to peace talks and violence with Israel over Palestinian state; oversaw Palestinian Liberation Organization, which pursued sovereignty and independence, often violently; photos (L)

2004-11-11.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Rare Brain Disease Confirmed
2004-11-11.abstract: Dublin hospital confirms first indigenous case of variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, incurable and degenerative brain disorder that is caused by eating meat infected with Mad Cow disease, in Ireland (S)

2004-11-11.title: Halliburton May Have Been Pressured by U.S. Diplomats to Disregard High Fuel Prices
2004-11-11.abstract: Documents released by Rep Henry A Waxman, Democrat and House Committee on Government Reform ranking minority member, show that American diplomats pressured Halliburton Company to keep using Altanmia, Kuwaiti subcontractor, despite unusually high prices for oil delivery services; Altanmia was hired by Halliburton subsidiary Kellogg Brown & Root, and prices for service were immediately questioned by critics in Congress, but defended by Halliburton; Pentagon is still considering possible call for refund and FBI is examing fuel transactions; some documents suggest American diplomats were eager to gain Kuwait's wider cooperation and provide greater fuel supplies to Iraq and in doing so rejected questions about Altanmia pricing (M)

2004-11-11.title: Tributes for Arafat Focus on Efforts for Palestinian State
2004-11-11.abstract: From the Vatican to the Palestinian refugee camps of Jordan, tributes poured in today for Yasir Arafat, a man regarded as an international leader by many and a terrorist by others.

2004-11-12.title: World Briefing | Europe: The Netherlands: Report Says 2 Terror Suspects Are Dutch-Americans
2004-11-12.abstract: Two Dutch-American brothers, who converted to Islam are among seven people arrested in antiterrorism raid in Netherlands; raid is part of continuing investigation into Islamic terrorist network linked with extremists in Morocco and Spain (S)

2004-11-12.title: Trials of G.I.'s at Abu Ghraib to Be Moved to the U.S.
2004-11-12.abstract: Military judge orders trials of soldiers accused of abuse at Iraq's Abu Ghraib prison to be moved to Fort Hood, Tex, from Baghdad; seven reservist soldiers from 372nd military police company based in Cresaptown, Maryland, stand accused of abuse (M)

2004-11-12.title: Whoosh! For Speeders, Speedier Justice, via Lamborghini
2004-11-12.abstract: Lamborghini spokesman Sergio Fontana discusses company's decision to donate Lamborghini Gallardo to Italian highway police to commemorate 152nd anniversary of national police force; Chief Inspector Laura Ciano and Superintendent Vincenzo Bizzarro, who have been trained to drive car, describe car's special features and experiences driving eye-catching police vehicle; Italy has road fatality rate higher than European average; officials admit speeders who are stopped by Gallardo are less angry when they receive ticket; photos (M)

2004-11-12.title: A grim report on future grabs Europe's attention
W.H.O. Panel Backs Gene Manipulation in Smallpox Virus
2004-11-12.abstract: World Health Organization takes first step toward approval of smallpox research that would involve gene manipulation; group advisory committee recommends that Russian and American high biosecurity labs perform research in hopes of finding drugs to treat disease; smallpox was eradicated in 1980, but concerns have grown that smallpox could become bioterrorism threat; so far, only drug cidofovir is candidate for treating smallpox; proposed study would involve insertion of green fluorescent market protein, gene, into variola virus (S)

2004-11-13.title: Names of the Dead
2004-11-13.abstract: Defense Department confirms deaths of 16 US servicemen in Iraq

2004-11-13.title: Dutch Hold 38 on Terror Charges After Raid on Camp
2004-11-13.abstract: Dutch police raid terrorist training camp for Kurdish separatists in Liempde; arrest 38 people there and at other places in country in ongoing crackdown on militants; are looking for Abu Khaled, Syrian who they say is leader of Islamic cell responsible for killing Dutch filmmaker Theo van Gogh; map (M)

2004-11-13.title: Arafat Is Buried in Chaotic Scene in the West Bank
2004-11-13.abstract: Yasir Arafat is buried in Ramallah, West Bank, in extraordinary scene of grief and chaos; thousands of Palestinians climb walls of his compound and surge around his coffin, trying to bear it aloft; Palestinian security guards are unable to hold back frenzied flow of some 20,000 mourners; passionate scene is in contrast to Arafat's Cairo funeral service earlier in day, which was restricted to officials and Arafat's wife Suha and daughter Zahwa, 9; photos (M)

2004-11-13.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: Taliban Leader Vows Return
2004-11-13.abstract: Mullah Mohammad Omar, deposed Taliban leader who is thought to be living in Pakistan and overseeing resistance movement, vows to take back control of Afghanistan (S)

2004-11-13.title: Globalist : The real cost of gold is far away, deep down

2004-11-14.title: Names of the Dead
2004-11-14.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of three American marines in Iraq, bringing total to 1,166

2004-11-14.title: Plant Shortage Leaves Campaigns Against Malaria at Risk
2004-11-14.abstract: Artemisinin, extract of sweet wormwood, is most cost-effective malaria treatment available, but shortages loom in 40 tropical countries that have made it centerpiece of their antimalarial efforts amid soaring demand and rising prices; plant is cultivated almost exclusively in China and Vietnam; multidrug combinations including artemisinin cost 10 to 20 times more than older malaria drugs but are more effective; Novartis's announcement that it will fall short on supply promised to World Health Organization provides most vivid evidence of problem; photo (M)

2004-11-14.title: Palestinians Schedule Election for Jan. 9
2004-11-14.abstract: Meanwhile, Mahmoud Abbas escaped injury as Palestinians waged a deadly gun battle during a mourning service for Yasir Arafat today.

2004-11-14.title: For Iraqi Leader, Political Risks of Attack on Falluja Grow
2004-11-14.abstract: Interim Iraqi Prime Min Ayad Allawi may have jeopardized political future by ordering attack on Falluja; both Sunnis and Shiites have condemned attack; militants kidnapped Alawi's cousin and his family on Nov 9, threatening to kill them if attack is not halted; Allawi is said to have insisted attack begin by Nov 12 so that there will be enough time to wrap it up by Nov 22, when he will attend conference in Egypt on Iraq's future (M)

2004-11-14.title: U.S. Armored Forces Blast Their Way Into Rebel Nest in Falluja
2004-11-14.abstract: Army tanks and fighting vehicles blast their way into last main rebel stronghold in Falluja at sundown after American warplanes and artillery prepare way with savage barrage on district; as battle intensifies in Falluja, insurgents roam streets of important northern city of Mosul and nearby town of Ramadi; photos (M)

2004-11-15.title: Workers Feel the Growing Pains of Russia's Auto Industry
2004-11-15.abstract: Izhavto automobile company in Izhevsk, Russia, is struggling to adapt to life under capitalism, and to laws of supply and demand; it recently shut down its plant and laid off 12,500 workers after running out of places to put its new cars, which it kept producing even as sales declined; map; graph (M)

2004-11-15.title: Raids in the Mosul Region Undermine Value of Victories
2004-11-15.abstract: Pitched battles erupt between insurgents and American and Iraqi forces in northern city of Mosul; carloads of insurgents drive unhindered through parts of city and attack security forces; hundreds of policemen in Mosul flee when attackers storm at least six police stations, taking American military by surprise; fighters barricade themselves in police station, wounding at least 20 Iraqi security commandos; thousands of Kurdish militiamen from outside Mosul begin taking up positions in streets; maps; caht (M)

2004-11-15.title: What the Rebels Left Behind: Arms, Grime, Death Lists
2004-11-15.abstract: Virtually every house that marine company goes through in Shuhada neighborhood of Falluja, considered final redoubt for insurgents, contains weapons, as well as booby traps and time bombs; they also find lists of names, as well as photographs and maps; map (M)

2004-11-15.title: Extremist parties unite for '06 ticket : The far right knocks at Bundestag's door
A Watchdog Follows the Money in Iraq
2004-11-15.abstract: Army Corps of Engineers is trying to demote Bunnatine H Greenhouse, its contracting director who has criticized corps for turning too readily and too generously to Halliburton Co for urgent repair of Iraqi oil fields; Greenhouse is determined to stay despite sarcastic remarks of her superiors about whistle-blowers; photo (M)

2004-11-16.title: U.N. Chief for Human Rights Raises Concern on Falluja
2004-11-16.abstract: Louise Arbour said today that she was concerned that the rules of war intended to protect civilians and combatants had been violated.

2004-11-16.title: Germany's far right knocks at Bundestag's door
Blair Sees England's Pubs as a Smoke-Free Zone
2004-11-16.abstract: By time-hallowed tradition, the English pub evokes an image of low beams, warm ales and the toxic swirling smog of tobacco smoke. But not, perhaps, for much longer.

2004-11-16.title: World Briefing | Asia: Putin Indicates Kurile Deal Is Not Likely Soon
2004-11-16.abstract: Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin plays down comments by Foreign Min Sergey V Lavrov that suggest Russia would give two of southernmost Kurile Islands to Japan; islands were seized by Russia at end of World War II and have been impediment to improved relations between nations (S)

2004-11-16.title: World Briefing | Europe: Serbia: Croatian Premier In First Visit
2004-11-16.abstract: Serbian Pres Svetozar Marovic welcomes Croatian Prime Min Ivo Sanader for first official visit by Croatian prime min since before 1990's war between nations; photo (S)

2004-11-17.title: Globalist : South Africa's ghosts haunt thinking on HIV
Palestinians Face Boycotts of Election for President
2004-11-17.abstract: Hamas and Islamic Jihad say they will not take part in Palestinian presidential election set for January, and Hamas says it will not suspend attacks against Israel; Prime Min Ariel Sharon indicates that if new Palestinian leadership cracks down on violence he would work on coordinating Gaza withdrawal plan; new PLO leader Mahmoud Abbas holds talks with Hamas leaders in Gaza (M)

2004-11-17.title: Sides in Falluja Fight for Hearts and Minds
2004-11-17.abstract: US in days before Falluja invasion dropped more than million leaflets urging insurgents not to fight and Iraqi police in streets gave out leaflets with lurid images warning that insurgents twist minds of Iraqi youth; photo; insurgents offered own propaganda, such as poster showing Statue of Liberty being pulled down; US military teams now clearing city will continue 'soft war' of promoting Iraqi security forces and government and publicizing reconstruction (M)

2004-11-17.title: Group Says Iran Has Secret Nuclear Arms Program
2004-11-17.abstract: Iranian opposition group says it has new evidence that Iran is producing enriched uranium at covert facility in Tehran that has not been disclosed to UN inspectors (S)

2004-11-17.title: U.S. Troops Move to Drive Out Rebels in North of Iraq
2004-11-17.abstract: US military races to contain spreading insurgency, sending hundreds of soldiers and armored vehicles into Mosul to root out bands of rebels who commandeered parts of city as Americans battled their way into Falluja; family of kidnapped aid worker Margaret Hassan believes she is woman shown being executed on videotape released by insurgents; photos; map; US military investigates videotaped fatal shooting of apparently wounded insurgent by marine in Falluja mosque; snipers continue shooting at troops roaming debris-covered streets on Falluja as residents emerge warily; rebels continue wave of attacks across Sunni Triangle, in Baquba and Ramadi, and bomb oil pipelines near Kirkuk; US soldiers is killed and another wounded by bomb near Baghdad (M)

2004-11-18.title: India's Offer for Peace Talks on Kashmir Is Sweetened With Aid
2004-11-18.abstract: Indian Prime Min Manmohan Singh, visiting Kashmir, offers unconditional talks with anyone willing to renounce violence and $5.3 billion economic package intended to 'win hearts and minds' of residents; plans to create 24,000 government jobs; visit comes as unspecified number of troops begin withdrawing from area in India's first declared force cut since anti-Indian insurgency began in 1989; Singh suggests there can be further troop cuts if situation approves and infiltration of militants declines; violence continues, with security forces killing two militants under mile from Singh's public meeting site; map (M)

2004-11-18.title: Monitors of North Korean News Note Dip in Reverence for Kim
2004-11-18.abstract: Portraits of leader Kim Jong Il reportedly have been removed from honored spots in North Korea and official news outlets are dropping honorific 'Dear Leader;' regional analysts debate whether he loses grip in power or, more likely, orchestrates downsizing of own personality cult (M)

2004-11-18.title: With purges in Myanmar, reform outlook dims
Chirac Says War in Iraq Spreads Terrorism
2004-11-18.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac says American-led invasion of Iraq has made world more dangerous, spurring Islamic terrorism around world, although Saddam Hussein's departure was positive to certain extent, British television interview; shows disdain for Bush administration and dismisses Prime Min Tony Blair's unyielding support for Bush, interview in British newspapers; photo (M)

2004-11-18.title: Growing importance of EU is lure : Lobbyists beat a path to power in Brussels

2004-11-19.title: Jailed in Israel, Palestinian Symbol Eyes Top Post
2004-11-19.abstract: Most popular Palestinian candidate for new leadership is Marwan Barghouti, who is considering run for presidency of Palestinian Authority from Israeli jail cell where he is serving five life terms on terrorism conviction; photos; Barghouti, 45, speaks Hebrew and English and has sophisticated understanding of Israelis, both from stints in prison and from formerly close ties to Israeli politicians; Israeli remember him for support of two-state solution and speculation abounds that he could be released now, but that is considered unlikely because court found he has blood on his hands from attacks of recent years; Barghouti is leader in rising generation of Palestinians who grew up under Israeli occupation and came to admire aspects of Israeli democracy; his wife Fadwa, 40-year-old lawyer, voices commonplace complaints about corruption under Yasir Arafat (M)

2004-11-19.title: Prince Charles Says He Knows His Place and Yours, Too
2004-11-19.abstract: London court hearing Elaine Day's suit over dismissal from Prince Charles's household staff hears memo that prince wrote in 2002 complaining about people who think they are 'qualified to do things far above their capabilities' and blaming 'learning culture' in schools that admits no failure by anyone; not clear is how former secretary got memo or whether it referred to her; photo; education official calls prince old-fashioned, but royal spokesman says Charles means that people have different talents to be developed (M)

2004-11-19.title: U.N. Council, in Nairobi, Again Warns Sudan
2004-11-19.abstract: UN Security Council convenes unusual session in Kenya to try to bring peace to neighboring Sudan; members agree on resolution that will suggest unspecified action against parties that do not cease hostilities in Darfur (S)

2004-11-19.title: Letter from America : Fear factor:Did Bush use it better than Kerry?
World Briefing | Europe: New European Commissioners
2004-11-19.abstract: European Union Parliament approves new executive branch to be led by incoming commissioner Jose Manuel Durao Barroso of Portugal (S)

2004-11-20.title: World Briefing | Africa: Congo: Annan Says Peacekeepers Abused Women And Children
2004-11-20.abstract: Fred Eckhard, United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan's spokesman, reports that there is clear evidence that some UN peacekeepers in Congo have committed acts of sexual abuse against women and children; team of investigators is being sent immediately; 70 complaints have been made and around 20 are actionable (S)

2004-11-20.title: Myanmar junta freeing some of its foes
U.S. Drops Effort for Treaty Banning Cloning
2004-11-20.abstract: US, faced with polarizing division in General Assembly, abandons its attempt to obtain UN treaty banning human cloning, including that done in name of medical research; outcome--agreement to come up with nonbinding declaration against cloning to reproduce humans--falls far short of US goal and represents setback for Pres Bush (M)

2004-11-20.title: Italians rally to keep Fiat 500 on the road
G.I.'s and Iraqis Raid Mosque, Killing 3
2004-11-20.abstract: US and Iraqi troops raid Abu Hanifa Mosque in Baghdad, killing at least three Iraqis; Iraqi commanders in Mosul stage numerous raids in search of rebel hideouts as up to dozen decapitated bodies are found strewn about city; map; photo (M)

2004-11-21.title: Bush Says Iran Speeds Output of A-Bomb Fuel
2004-11-21.abstract: Pres Bush, on first day of meeting of Pacific Rim leaders in Santiago, Chile, steps up pressure on Iran over nuclear weapons production even as he tries to establish unified front against nuclear challenge from North Korea; meets with leaders of China, Japan and South Korea, urging each to draw North Korea back into six-nation negotiations; issues direct challenge to Kim Jong Il to get rid of his weapons program; calls indications that Iran is accelerating its uranium enrichment program 'a very serious matter' that undercuts Iran's denials that it is seeking to build weapons; photo (M)

2004-11-21.title: In Falluja, Young Marines Saw the Savagery of an Urban War
2004-11-21.abstract: Eight-day siege of Falluja is most sustained period of street-to-street fighting that American troops have encountered in Iraq, but is probably kind they will see again; price for Americans is 51 dead and 425 wounded, number that may yet increase but that already exceeds toll from any other battle; savagery of urban warfare and grinding struggle to root out guerrillas as experienced by Bravo Company of First Battalion, Eighth Marines, described in detail; company of 150 took 36 casualties, including 6 dead; map; photos (M)

2004-11-21.title: Martin Kaplan, 89, Health Official Who Fought the Spread of Disease, Dies
2004-11-21.abstract: Martin M Kaplan, virologist, international public health official and humanitarian who spent his early career researching spread of viral diseases and his later career working to fight spread of nuclear, biological and chemical weapons, dies at age 89; photo (M)/

2004-11-21.title: Commuter Plane Crashes in China, Killing 54
2004-11-21.abstract: The Bombardier CRJ-200 aircraft plunged into the lake in Nanhai Park just seconds after taking off from Baotou in the Chinese region of Inner Mongolia.

2004-11-21.title: Rebels Keep Up Attacks in Central and North Iraq
2004-11-21.abstract: Violence surges throughout central and northern Iraq as insurgency led by Sunni Arabs keeps up assault in several cities; American offensive in Falluja continues, while marines engage in holding action around Ramadi after setting up blockade; photos (M)

2004-11-22.title: Security Dispute Dulls Luster of Bush's Trip to Chile
2004-11-22.abstract: United States Pres George W Bush, who is attending Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation annual meeting in Santiago, Chile, receives cool reception from Chilean people; Chilean government disinvites more than 200 guests to large state dinner because they do not want other guests subjected to US security in order to enter Chilean government buildings for event; disagreement between Secret Service agent and Chilean police at dinner with leaders of 21 APEC leaders requires Pres Bush to escort his security into event; photos (M)

2004-11-22.title: Powell Says Israel Will Ease Strictures for Palestinian Vote
2004-11-22.abstract: Israel promised to ease conditions in the West Bank and Gaza so that Palestinians can vote for a successor to Yasir Arafat.

2004-11-22.title: Iran to Suspend Uranium Enrichment Today
2004-11-22.abstract: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Hamidreza Assefi reports that Iran will suspend uranium enrichment beginning Nov 22 as agreed during meeting earlier in month with Britain, Germany and France; confirms suspension in letter to International Atomic Energy Agency; United States has accused Iran of increasing production just before suspension in order to stockpile as much uranium hexafluoride as possible; Gholamreza Aghazadeh, Iran's Atomic Energy Organization head, denies US charges (M)

2004-11-22.title: Can Joschka Make the Warrior Princess smile?
2004-11-22.abstract: German Foreign Minister Joschka Fischer is famous for charming his diplomat colleagues. Condoleezza Rice is famous for not being charmable. How will the two get along?

2004-11-22.title: Bush, in Colombia, Says He Will Seek More U.S. Antidrug Funds
2004-11-22.abstract: The president did not mention a specific sum, but he was effusive in his praise of the Colombian leader's antidrug efforts.

2004-11-23.title: Iraq Falls Short on Vote Security
2004-11-23.abstract: Iraq has far fewer security officers than needed to protect 9,000 polling places during Jan elections; many of its officers have performed poorly in Sunni Arab areas where worst violencce is expected; role American troops will play is still being defined, though they will probably serve as backups out of sight of polling stations; Sen Jack Reed note election commission has not shared data on polling places, making it hard for American commanders to set up protection plan; photo (M)

2004-11-23.title: After 8 Years in Jail, Husband of Bhutto Is Free
2004-11-23.abstract: Asif Ali Zardari, husband of former Pakistani Prime Min Benazir Bhutto, is freed onbail on last of numerous corruption charges that have kept him in prison since 1996; elated opposition party members celebrate his release outside hospital, Karachi, where he has been undergoing medical treatment; government officials say they hope release will ease tensions with opposition; photo (M)

2004-11-23.title: Lacking Doctors, Africa Is Training Substitutes
2004-11-23.abstract: Trained health care workers are doing work of doctors in rural clinics in Ethiopa and elsewhere in Africa, diagnosing and treating major killers; international health specialists say training more such paraprofessionals could offer solution to problem vexing poor African nations: brain drain of doctors to rich Western nations and more prosperous African ones; few rigorous studies have found technicians provide decent care; map; photo (M)

2004-11-23.title: Names of the Dead
2004-11-23.abstract: List of American service members newly confirmed dead in Iraq

2004-11-23.title: Arafat's Death Remains a Mystery, Nephew Says After Seeing Records
2004-11-23.abstract: Yasir Arafat's nephew, Nasser al-Kidwa, says medical records released to him by French Defense Ministry show doctors found no traces of known poisons in Arafat before he died, news conference, Paris; says cause of death remains mystery; will turn records over to Palestinian Authority for review (M)

2004-11-24.title: UN faults Croatia on war crimes suspect, dimming EU prospects
3 Kidnapped U.N. Workers Are Released in Kabul
2004-11-24.abstract: Kidnappers free three United Nations workers held since Oct 28 in apparently orchestrated handover, Kabul, Afghanistan; Min Ahmed Ali Jalali denies deal was made with kidnappers; says release follows recent series of police and American-led military operations in and around Kabul; says Taliban splinter group Jaish-e-Muslimeen, which claimed responsibility and threatened to kill hostages, probably had hand in kidnapping; group's leader, Akbar Agha, insists deal was made and that Taliban prisoners will be freed; hostages, Annetta Flanigan, Shqipe Hebibi and Angelito Nayan, are in good health; photos (M)

2004-11-24.title: With Russia eyeing euro, 'more fuel' on dollar fire
Names of the Dead
2004-11-24.abstract: List of American service members newly confired dead in Iraq

2004-11-24.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Minister Says Berlusconi Would Lose Early Elections
2004-11-24.abstract: Italian European Affairs Minister Rocco Buttiglione holds that Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi would not win early election if he resigned over proposed tax cuts; Berlusconi has threatened to resign if his tax reductions are not enacted (S)

2004-11-25.title: Dutch Try to Thwart Terror Without Being Overzealous
2004-11-25.abstract: His telephone was tapped, his apartment was watched and many of his friends were already behind bars, so the Dutch authorities were not surprised by evidence that it was Mohamed Bouyeri, a Dutchman of Moroccan descent, who murdered the filmmaker Theo van Gogh in broad daylight one morning this month. Yet they had been powerless to stop the crime.     It is a problem faced by most European governments as radical Islam spreads across the Continent: how to arrest suspected militants before they act, without trampling on individual rights or risking charges of discrimination.

2004-11-25.title: Tape Condemns Sunni Muslim Clerics
2004-11-25.abstract: Audiotape is posted on Internet in which man identified as Abu Musab al-Zarqawi, Jordanian terrorist leader, condemns Sunni Muslim clerics for abandoning Iraqi resistance movement in face of US military offensive in Falluja and other Sunni cities; photo (M)

2004-11-25.title: Lynchings of Policemen Ignite Outrage at Violence in Mexico
2004-11-25.abstract: Mexico City seems in state of shock on Nov 24, as people struggle to come to grips with scenes of lawlessness captured on television as angry mob lynched two police officers while hundreds of officers surrounded area but failed to intervene; killings in San Juan Ixtayopan, poor settlement, has set off new wave of outrage over violent crime and failures of corrupt justice system (M)

2004-11-25.title: Being 'clear,' EU keeps pressure on Turkey
Sudan Rebels Say Government Bombed Village Camp, Killing 25
2004-11-25.abstract: Sudanese Liberation Army commander Suleiman Jamous says government warplane bombed rebel camp in Tadit, killing 25 fighters; says rebels vacated Tawilah, but denies capturing town in first place, as government and UN officials had said earlier (M)

2004-11-26.title: Ukraine Court Delays Results in Vote Dispute
2004-11-26.abstract: Ukraine Supreme Court temporarily blocks victory of Prime Min Viktor F Yanukovich in disputed presidential election until court reviews complaints of fraud and abuse; unexpected ruling shifts momentum toward loser, Viktor A Yushchenko and thousands of demonstrators in Kiev; photo of Ukrainian police guards on fifth day of opposition demonstrations (M)

2004-11-26.title: After lunch in Texas, it's quiet in Madrid
EU pressure intensifies for reforms in Turkey
Bloc of Militia in Colombia Lays Down Arms
2004-11-26.abstract: Some 450 paramilitaries in Colombia's northern region disarm on Nov 25; it is among first of several demobilizations intended to take 3,000 paramilitary fighters out of chaotic conflict in Colombia (M)

2004-11-27.title: Despite Pact, New Violence Stymies Aid in Sudan
2004-11-27.abstract: Fresh surge of violence is engulfing Darfur just two weeks after Sudanese authorities and their guerrilla enemies signed preliminary peace agreement, with rebels sharply ratcheting up attacks, government offering heavy-handed retaliation and large patches of land becoming closed to relief efforts; 1,000 African Union troops in Darfur are authorized to do little more than monitor cease-fire violations; map; photos (M)

2004-11-27.title: A rhapsody in East European crafts
Conflict Over Madrid's Last Remaining Statue of Franco Never Dies
2004-11-27.abstract: Pressure is building on Madrid officials to remove city's last remaining statue of Francisco Franco, 29 years after his death; Mayor Alberto Ruiz-Gallardon says he cannot take down statue of fascist leader because it is not city property; photo (M)

2004-11-27.title: Letter from Asia : Mongolian heavy metal leaves Chinese panicky
for Asia antique lovers : Quality reproductions

2004-11-28.title: Pakistan to Scale Back Force Around Town Near Afghan Border
2004-11-28.abstract: Pakistan will withdraw troops from forward positions and checkpoints around Wana in South Waziristan, saying peace has been restored, although foreign fighters still wander area in large numbers; government says it has killed hundreds of militants since March, but has lost 202 of its own troops; military has reached deal in which local militants receive amnesty in exchange for renouncing attacks and support of foreign militants; agreement does not cover defiant Mehsud tribe (M)

2004-11-28.title: Hut by Hut, AIDS Steals Life in a Southern Africa Town
2004-11-28.abstract: Article A Hollowed Generation describes how AIDS is steadily boring through heart of one small town in Africa, stealing life and breeding desperation as it is doing across continent; Lavumisa, Swaziland, has lost parents, teachers and health workers who leave behind destitute orphans with few healthy kin to care for them; tiny town of 2,000 is template for demographic plunge in every corner of southern Africa as AIDS reduces life expectancy to levels of 1800's; average child born today will not live to 40; photos; map; charts; terrible ripples that unrestrained epidemic sends through small village described; schools collapse, crime rises, medical clinics are jammed and family assets are sold to fend off hunger because adults are dead, and their deaths have robbed survivors of their own aspirations (L)

2004-11-28.title: Iranian Retreat Appears to Salvage Nuclear Agreement
2004-11-28.abstract: Iran backed off a demand to operate equipment that could be used either for energy purposes or in a nuclear bomb-making project.

2004-11-28.title: Iran Reasserts Its Right to Enrich Uranium as Standoff Persists
2004-11-28.abstract: Iran reasserts its right to retain 20 centrifuges for enriching uranium for research purposes, dashing hopes of Britain, Germany and France that they had negotiated compromise that would avoid confrontation in United Nations; there is still hope that Iran might agree verbally to yield on centrifuges, although perhaps not in writing; United States believes Iran is trying to develop nuclear weapons (M)

2004-11-28.title: U.N. Tackles Issue of Imbalance of Power
2004-11-28.abstract: International panel will publish recommendations on how to update United Nations for 21st century challenges; report is said to recommend expansion of Security Council to 24 members, with six each from Americas, Africa, Asia and Europe, suggesting two different options for implementation, neither of which extends veto power beyond five existing permanent members; changes to Security Council must be approved by two-thirds of General Assembly's 191 members; debate is expected to be intense (M)

2004-11-29.title: Shadow of Vietnam Falls Over Iraq River Raids
2004-11-29.abstract: Marines in river boats searching for Iraqi insurgents conjure memories of another American war, in Vietnam; troops in high-tech updates on Swift boats used in Vietnam move up wide rivers, with machine-gunners nervously scanning riverbanks for hidden enemy; river raid on village of Chard Duwaish is part of weeklong offensive billed as sequel to battle for Falluja, just 20 miles away; American commanders are eager to exploit potential patrol boats give them for mounting fast, unexpected attacks along Tigris and Euphrates; raids are also chance to exploit another new dimension for winning war: twinning American combat units with newly trained Iraqi troops; although recruits in new Iraqi units undergo strict vetting, American officers say rebel sympathizers have infiltrated some units, and some of soldiers have been caught tipping off rebel groups; photos; map (M)

2004-11-29.title: Southeast Asia Urged to Form Economic Bloc
2004-11-29.abstract: Two of Southeast Asia's newest leaders, Indonesia's Susilo Bambang Yudhoyono and Malaysia's Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, call for faster economic integration at annual summit meeting of 10-member Assn of Southeast Asian Nations; dominant theme for association has become how to handle China, which along with Japan, South Korea and India sends representatives to annual session; fear of being overcome by China has driven group to move toward economic integration, with some talking of eventual pan-Asian economic community resembling European Union (M)

2004-11-29.title: Democrats call on Bush to break 9/11 bill impasse
U.N. Agency Welcomes Iran's Nuclear Halt
2004-11-29.abstract: The I.A.E.A. welcomed Iran's suspension of its nuclear program but said it would continue to monitor it.

2004-11-30.title: U.S. Officials Say Iraq's Forces Founder Under Rebel Assaults
2004-11-30.abstract: American officials say Iraqi police and national guard forces, whose performance is crucial to securing Jan elections, are foundering in face of coordinated efforts to kill and intimidate them and their families; Iraqi recruits have been victims of assassinations and car bombs aimed at lines of applicants as well as police stations; in latest attack, suicide bomber rammed car into group of police officers west of Ramadi, killing 12 people; local American commanders and security officials say both Iraqi forces are riddled with problems; say that in most violent provinces, they are so intimidated that many are reluctant to show up and do not tell their families where they work; say they have not yet received adequate training or weapons, and they present danger to American troops they fight alongside; say they are unreliable because of corruption, desertion or infiltration; Lt Gen David H Petraeus, American commander overseeing training of Iraqi security forces, acknowledges shortcomings in Iraqis' performance, particularly by police in Mosul and in Anbar Province; says they have done well in Falluja, Najaf, Kut, Hilla, Karbala and much of southern Iraq, where security situation is not as dangerous; photo (M)

2004-11-30.title: World Briefing
2004-11-30.abstract: CANADA: BEEF ACCORD EXPECTED; CHILE: PAYMENT FOR TORTURE VICTIMS; ISRAEL: SHARON SQUEAKER IN PARLIAMENT; SPAIN: AZNAR DEFENDS ACTIONS AFTER MADRID BOMBINGS; RUSSIA: DALAI LAMA ARRIVES; CHINA: HOPES FADE FOR MINERS

2004-11-30.title: Aznar defends decision to link Madrid blasts to ETA
Board Accepts Nuclear Vow by Iranians
2004-11-30.abstract: Ruling board of International Atomic Energy Agency passes mildly worded resolution welcoming Iran's total freeze on sensitive part of its nuclear program; decision by 35-country board seen as defeat for Bush administration, which repeatedly tried to persuade fellow board members to debate Iran's case in Security Council before deciding to go along with board's decision; resolution, passed by consensus without vote, removes possibility that Iran will be dragged before Security Council for possible censure or even sanctions; Jackie Wolcott Sanders, head of American delegation, says US could decide unilaterally to send Iran case before Security Council, move opposed by Britain, Germany, Russia, China and other countries; photos (M)

2004-11-30.title: Opposition Leader in Ukraine Rejects Offer by Official Winner
2004-11-30.abstract: Prime Minister Viktor F. Yanukovich offered to not run in a new election, as long as his challenger also stepped aside.
